notable.notable_blurb: Engineer; historian of American machine tools

narrative_blocks.header: He wrote the book on American toolmakers
narrative_blocks.body: Roe's 1916 study English and American Tool Builders became the classic history of the men who built America's machine tools. A Yale-trained engineer, he later chaired industrial engineering at New York University.

narrative_blocks.header: He flew with the wartime Signal Corps
narrative_blocks.body: During the First World War Roe served as a major in the Aviation Section of the Army Signal Reserve Corps. He returned to consult for the Navy in the Second World War.

narrative_blocks.header: He wrote the history of machine tools
narrative_blocks.body: Trained at Yale's Sheffield Scientific School, Roe became a pioneering professor of industrial engineering at Yale and New York University. His 1916 book English and American Tool Builders became the classic history of the machine-tool trade.

narrative_blocks.header: His book named the great toolmakers
narrative_blocks.body: Roe traced the lineage of American precision manufacturing through the New England armories and press-builders like the Hoe dynasty. Generations of engineers learned their craft's history from his pages.

Roe helped establish industrial engineering as an academic discipline, teaching at Yale's Sheffield School and later at New York University. His students carried his methods into American factories during the era of scientific management.

INST0044.name: Yale Sheffield Scientific School
INST0044.notes: Founded 1847 as the Yale Department of Philosophy and the Arts; became the Sheffield Scientific School 1861. Merged fully into Yale College 1956. Offered 3-year programs in engineering and applied science at lower cost than Yale College. Distinct from the "Yale" of liberal arts and law — Sheffield produced engineers, industrialists, and scientists. Notable Hooker-connected alumni: Joseph Wickham Roe (HD2556, 1895) — professor of industrial engineering, NYU; author of "English and American Tool Builders" (1916). Roe family papers at Yale: MS 774.
